steps.observed: Section 6 covers alloc, generation rollover, free (4 sites + Drop), end-to-end trace, and 4 invariants

steps.action: Grep kernel source for all address_space_id alloc/free call sites
steps.expected: Every alloc has a matching free in process cleanup
steps.observed: 1 alloc site (process.rs:102), 5 explicit free sites + 1 Drop safety net = 6 total free paths covering all process lifecycle scenarios

steps.action: Verify generation rollover path in address_space_id.rs
steps.expected: TLB flush before ASID reuse on exhaustion
steps.observed: Lines 65-75: DSB ISHST + TLBI VMALLE1IS + DSB ISH + ISB executed before generation increment and bitmap reset

steps.action: Verify TLB invalidation before free on all exit/kill paths
steps.observed: All 5 explicit free paths preceded by invalidate_tlb() call

issues.description: Safety-map free sites table has label mismatches: syscall.rs:783 labeled as sys_process_kill (actually sys_process_create rollback), syscall.rs:855 labeled as exit_current_from_syscall (actually sys_process_kill). Additionally, scheduler.rs:1000 (actual exit_current_from_syscall) is missing from the table. The safety-map says '4 free sites' but there are actually 5 explicit free call sites. Despite these labeling issues, the functional coverage is complete — every alloc has a matching free.

steps.observed: Section 7 documents 5 increment sites and 6 decrement categories with release_context_inner and release_thread_context_ids implementations

steps.action: Grep kernel source for all ref_count operations in scheduler.rs
steps.expected: All documented sites match actual code
steps.observed: All 5 increment sites verified: create(836), init(1038), bind(638), borrow(717), bind_default(166). All 6 decrement categories verified: release_sc(1241), return_sc(1262), exit_current(910,912), kill_process ready/blocked/suspended(~1115,1135,1155), kill running(~1199), categorize_handles(188)

steps.expected: saturating_sub prevents underflow, freed exactly when ref_count=0
steps.observed: scheduler.rs:279 uses saturating_sub(1), line 281 checks ==0 to set slot to None and push to free_context_ids

steps.action: Verify ref_count reaches zero iff all handles closed AND all threads exited
steps.observed: Both exit_current_from_syscall and kill_process paths release all per-thread refs. Handle drain releases handle refs via categorize_handles. All paths converge on release_context_inner.
Both values are 8. Stack allocation = 4096 * 8 = 32 KiB. Match confirmed.

steps.expected: Section on emergency stack sizing present
steps.observed: Section 8 documents allocation, constant, verification table, stack selection logic, historical bug, and invariant

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 3 passed. VAL-INVAR-006 (ASID lifecycle) has minor documentation label mismatches in the safety-map free sites table but functional coverage is complete. VAL-INVAR-007 (ref_count) and VAL-INVAR-008 (stack sizing) fully verified with no issues. All 1054 tests pass.
